text: Which sorting algorithm is stable and works efficiently for linked lists?
What is the worst-case time complexity of Bubble Sort?
Which sorting algorithm is not comparison-based?
Which sorting algorithm is adaptive and can run in O(n) time on nearly sorted arrays?
Which sorting algorithm uses divide-and-conquer and merges two sorted halves?
What is the best-case time complexity of Insertion Sort?
Which sorting algorithm always performs O(n^2) comparisons regardless of input order?
Which property is true for Selection Sort when sorting an array of n elements?
options: Runs in O(n log n) time

text: Which algorithm is stable by default (preserves relative order of equal elements)?
Which algorithm uses adjacent exchanges to push the largest element to the end of the array each pass?
Which sorting method repeatedly finds the minimum element and places it at the beginning?
Optimized Bubble Sort (stops when no swaps occur) has best-case time complexity of:
Which algorithm is typically in-place (O(1) extra space ignoring recursion stack)?
Which algorithm has average O(n log n) time but O(n^2) worst case without careful pivot selection?
How many comparisons does Selection Sort perform overall to sort n elements (asymptotically)?
Which stable linear-time subroutine is commonly used inside Radix Sort for each digit?
Which statement about stability is correct?
options: In-place Quick Sort is always stable
Merge Sort can be stable if implemented accordingly
answer: Merge Sort can be stable if implemented accordingly

text: When keys are small non-negative integers and linear time is desired, which algorithm is best suited?
Which statement describes the Merge vs Quick trade-off most accurately?
options: Merge uses O(1) space and guarantees O(n log n); Quick uses O(n) space
Merge uses O(n) space and guarantees O(n log n); Quick is in-place but can be O(n^2)
Quick guarantees O(n log n) worst case; Merge is in-place
Both always use O(1) space and O(n log n) time
answer: Merge uses O(n) space and guarantees O(n log n); Quick is in-place but can be O(n^2)

text: Which sorting algorithm is best suited for sorting large datasets that do not fit in memory?
Which of the following sorting algorithms is not stable in its standard implementation?
What is the primary advantage of using Heap Sort over Quick Sort?
options: Heap Sort has a better worst-case time complexity
Heap Sort is easier to implement
answer: Heap Sort has a better worst-case time complexity

text: Which sorting algorithm can be described as a combination of Insertion Sort and Merge Sort?
In which scenario does Quick Sort outperform Merge Sort?
options: When the array is nearly sorted
When the array is sorted in reverse order
When there are many duplicate elements
When the array size is very large
answer: When the array is nearly sorted

text: Which of the following is a characteristic of Shell Sort?
options: It uses a gap sequence to reduce inversions
It requires additional memory for merging
answer: It uses a gap sequence to reduce inversions

text: What is the main purpose of the 'gap' in Shell Sort?
options: To divide the array into smaller sub-arrays
To determine the position of elements in the final sorted array
To reduce the number of inversions
answer: To reduce the number of inversions

text: Which sorting algorithm is likely to be the fastest in practice for small arrays due to low overhead?
Which of the following sorting algorithms can be implemented using a binary tree?
What is the primary disadvantage of using Bubble Sort?
options: It has poor worst-case time complexity
answer: It has poor worst-case time complexity

text: Which sorting algorithm is considered the 'divide and conquer' algorithm?
Which of the following is true about Counting Sort?
options: It is not a stable sort

text: Which sorting algorithm would be best suited for a situation where memory space is extremely limited?
What is the main advantage of Merge Sort over other O(n log n) algorithms?
options: It is stable and works well with linked lists
answer: It is stable and works well with linked lists

text: Which of the following sorting algorithms is not typically used for external sorting?
What is the primary use of Radix Sort?
options: Sorting integers or strings by individual digits
Sorting based on the frequency of elements
Sorting with a focus on minimizing comparisons
Sorting elements with a known, limited range of values
answer: Sorting integers or strings by individual digits

text: Which of the following best describes Tim Sort?
options: A hybrid of Merge Sort and Insertion Sort
A hybrid of Quick Sort and Heap Sort
A stable version of Bubble Sort
An adaptive version of Selection Sort
answer: A hybrid of Merge Sort and Insertion Sort

text: In the context of sorting algorithms, what does the term 'in-place' mean?
options: The algorithm does not use any extra memory
The algorithm sorts the data without modifying the original array
The algorithm requires a constant amount of space
The algorithm is implemented directly within the data structure
answer: The algorithm requires a constant amount of space

text: Which sorting algorithm is most sensitive to the choice of pivot?
What is the main goal of using a sentinel in a sorting algorithm?
options: To mark the end of the array
To simplify the code by avoiding boundary checks
To indicate the position of the last sorted element
answer: To simplify the code by avoiding boundary checks

text: Which of the following sorting algorithms can be made stable by a simple modification?
What is the effect of using a bad pivot in Quick Sort?
options: It can make the algorithm unstable
It can increase the time complexity to O(n^2)
answer: It can increase the time complexity to O(n^2)

text: Which sorting algorithm would be the best choice for a nearly sorted array?
Which of the following is a disadvantage of Merge Sort?
options: It requires additional memory for the merge
It is slower than Quick Sort in all cases
answer: It requires additional memory for the merge

text: What is the primary purpose of the partitioning step in Quick Sort?
options: To divide the array into two halves
To arrange elements around a pivot
To find the median of the array
answer: To arrange elements around a pivot

text: Which of the following sorting algorithms has the smallest worst-case runtime?
Which sorting algorithm is most commonly used in practice for general-purpose sorting and why?
options: Quick Sort, because it is the fastest on average
Merge Sort, because it is the most stable
Heap Sort, because it has the best worst-case performance
Insertion Sort, because it is the simplest to implement
answer: Quick Sort, because it is the fastest on average

text: Which of the following is true about the space complexity of Merge Sort?
Which sorting algorithm can be described as a 'non-comparison based' sorting algorithm?
What is the main characteristic of a stable sorting algorithm?
options: It maintains the relative order of records with equal keys
answer: It maintains the relative order of records with equal keys

text: Which of the following sorting algorithms is considered adaptive?
Which sorting algorithm is generally not affected by the initial order of elements in the array?
Which of the following is a key advantage of using Counting Sort?
options: It is very fast for small ranges of integers
answer: It is very fast for small ranges of integers

text: What is the main disadvantage of Counting Sort?
options: It requires a large amount of memory for large ranges
It is slower than comparison-based sorts
answer: It requires a large amount of memory for large ranges

text: Which sorting algorithm is most commonly used for sorting strings?
What is the primary benefit of using Tim Sort in practice?
options: It is the fastest sorting algorithm
It is a stable sort that works well on many kinds of real-world data
It is the simplest sorting algorithm to implement
It uses the least amount of memory
answer: It is a stable sort that works well on many kinds of real-world data

text: Which of the following best describes the performance of Heap Sort?
options: O(n log n) on average, O(n^2) worst case
O(n) on average, O(n^2) worst case

text: Which sorting algorithm is least likely to be affected by the presence of duplicate elements?
What is the main purpose of the 'merge' step in Merge Sort?
options: To combine two sorted arrays into a single sorted array
To divide the array into smaller sub-arrays
To find the median of the array
To remove duplicates from the array
answer: To combine two sorted arrays into a single sorted array

What is the primary challenge in implementing Quick Sort?
Which of the following is a common optimization for Merge Sort?
options: Using insertion sort for small subarrays
answer: Using insertion sort for small subarrays

text: What is the effect of using a hybrid sorting algorithm?
options: It combines the advantages of different algorithms
answer: It combines the advantages of different algorithms

What is the primary advantage of using introspective sort (IntroSort)?

To combine the strengths of different sorting algorithms
